instruction: Enter the quality issue into the quality ledger

command: DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true

printf 'Quality Issue Report
Issue ID: QI-2025-0089
Occurrence Time: 2025-03-11
Process: Painting Line
Description: Color variation on product surface, ~15% non-uniformity rate
Affected Batches: B20250310A, B20250310B
Root Cause: Paint viscosity parameter set too low
Corrective Action: Adjust paint formula, recalibrate parameters
Responsible Person: Painting Supervisor Lu Yuan' > /home/user/Desktop/quality_issue.txt

python3 -c "import openpyxl; wb = openpyxl.Workbook(); ws = wb.active; ws['A1'] = 'Issue ID'; ws['B1'] = 'Process'; ws['C1'] = 'Description'; ws['D1'] = 'Affected Batches'; ws['E1'] = 'Root Cause'; ws['F1'] = 'Corrective Action'; ws['G1'] = 'Responsible'; wb.save('/home/user/Desktop/quality_log.xlsx')"

phases.instruction: Enter the quality issue into the quality ledger

Fill the quality issue information from /home/user/Desktop/quality_issue.txt into /home/user/Desktop/quality_log.xlsx row 2: A2='QI-2025-0089', B2='Painting Line', C2='Surface color variation', D2='B20250310A, B20250310B', E2='Paint viscosity parameter set too low', F2='Adjust paint formula', G2='Lu Yuan', save

result.command: python3 -c "import pandas as pd; df = pd.read_excel('/home/user/Desktop/quality_log.xlsx'); s = df.to_string(); print('PASSED' if 'QI-2025-0089' in s else 'FAILED')"
